name: rad-session
description: Three-phase Claude Code workflow lifecycle with cross-machine continuity and slim wrapup: /init bootstraps a project once (deterministic stack detection via Python scripts, CLAUDE.md scaffold, gitignore handling for session files, rad-* plugin recommendations), /startup orients each session (Phase 0 fetches origin and prompts to pull when behind BEFORE reading any handoff file — listing incoming commits first; --auto-pull / --no-pull flag overrides; cross-machine handoff detection via hostname; parallel-batched resource discovery; stale-handoff auto-refresh from git log), /wrapup captures each session (recency-bounded conversation tagging, structured HANDOFF.md with 'what NOT to do' field, mechanical session-log derivation from HANDOFF, CLAUDE.md prune auto-skipped when unchanged, Phase 6 auto-commit + prompted push of session files with --push / --no-push / --quick overrides), /add-resource registers tools any time. Two Python scripts (detect-stack.py, detect-resources.py) make stack and MCP detection deterministic. PreCompact hook prevents silent context loss. 3.4 closes the silent-skip-of-maintenance defect: Phase 3 is split into 3.A (append) and 3.B (mandatory programmatic Bash-gated maintenance — never conditional on LLM judgment), per-bullet length caps added to HANDOFF (≤3 sentences) and session-log (≤1 sentence per bullet, hard cap 30 lines / 1.5 KB per entry), and Phase 6 final state assertion makes oversized files / over-cap log impossible to hide.
